name: Von Neumann Algebras & Modular Theory in Quantum Gravity

concepts.name: von Neumann algebra type (I / II / III)
concepts.definition: Murray-von Neumann classification of factors by the range of dimensions/traces of their projections. Type I (B(H), ordinary QM, pure states, density matrices) has a trace with discrete range; type II has a continuous trace but no minimal (rank-one) projections, so it possesses density matrices and well-defined relative entropy but no pure states or 'bits'; type III has no trace at all and no density matrices, so entanglement entropy is intrinsically UV-divergent. The type is the precise mathematical fingerprint of how entanglement and entropy behave in a system.

concepts.name: Type III factor
concepts.definition: A von Neumann factor with no trace and no minimal projections, so it admits no density matrices and entanglement entropy is intrinsically UV-divergent. Murray-von Neumann/Connes class III (refined into III_lambda, 0<=lambda<=1, by the modular spectrum). Local relativistic QFT subregion algebras are type III_1 (Araki; Buchholz-Fredenhagen-Haag). Adjoining the modular automorphism group (crossed product) converts a type III_1 factor into type II with a trace.

concepts.name: Type II factor
concepts.definition: A von Neumann factor with a (up to scale unique) faithful semifinite trace but no minimal projections; it possesses density matrices and a finite, well-defined relative/von Neumann entropy fixed only up to a state-independent additive constant, yet has no pure states or 'bits'. Type II_1 has a finite normalized trace (bounded entropy, e.g. de Sitter static patch); type II_infinity a semifinite trace (e.g. black-hole / Killing-horizon observer algebras). Manufactured from a type III_1 algebra by the crossed product with its modular flow; renormalized von Neumann entropy equals generalized entropy.

concepts.name: Type III_1 of local QFT algebras
concepts.definition: A central structural theorem of algebraic QFT (Araki; Buchholz-Fredenhagen-Haag; Bisognano-Wichmann): the von Neumann algebra of observables localized in any bounded region or wedge of a relativistic quantum field theory is a type III_1 factor (in Connes' classification). Consequences: no states are uncorrelated across the boundary, every vector is cyclic and separating (Reeh-Schlieder), there are no local density matrices, and entanglement entropy of a subregion is UV-divergent and not directly definable. Gravity/an observer must be added to regulate it.

concepts.name: Tomita-Takesaki modular theory
concepts.definition: Given a von Neumann algebra M with a cyclic and separating vector |Omega>, the closure of the antilinear map S a|Omega> = a^dagger|Omega> has polar decomposition S = J Delta^{1/2}. The modular operator Delta and modular conjugation J satisfy Delta^{it} M Delta^{-it} = M (the modular automorphism / modular flow sigma_t) and J M J = M' (the commutant). This canonically extracts a one-parameter time flow and a duality between an algebra and its commutant purely from a state, with no external Hamiltonian. The deep input to thermal time, emergent geometry and gravitational entropy.

concepts.name: Modular Hamiltonian
concepts.definition: The self-adjoint generator K = -log Delta of the modular flow sigma_t = Delta^{it} (.) Delta^{-it}. For a thermal/reduced state it plays the role of an entanglement (or 'entanglement') Hamiltonian; modular flow is generically nonlocal, but for the Rindler wedge in the vacuum (Bisognano-Wichmann) it is the local Lorentz boost and K = 2 pi (boost generator), giving the Unruh temperature. Variations of expectation values of K control the first law of entanglement and relative entropy.

concepts.name: Modular flow (modular automorphism group)
concepts.definition: The one-parameter group sigma_t^omega of automorphisms of M generated by the modular Hamiltonian of a state omega. It is intrinsic to the pair (M, omega) and independent of the cyclic-separating representative. Geometric in special cases (boost for wedges, dilations near a point), generically nonlocal. Its status as a state-dependent but algebra-intrinsic 'time' underlies the thermal time hypothesis and half-sided modular inclusions.

concepts.name: KMS condition
concepts.definition: The Kubo-Martin-Schwinger analyticity condition <A sigma_t(B)> = <sigma_{t-i beta}(B) A> characterizing thermal equilibrium of a state with respect to a flow at inverse temperature beta. Tomita-Takesaki shows every cyclic-separating state is KMS at beta=1 with respect to its own modular flow: equilibrium and modular time are two views of the same structure. Identifies the restriction of the Minkowski vacuum to a wedge as a thermal state (Unruh).

concepts.name: Connes classification of type III factors
concepts.definition: Connes (1973) refined type III into a continuum III_lambda, 0 <= lambda <= 1, using the modular spectrum S(M) (intersection of Arveson spectra of all modular operators), a modular invariant independent of the chosen state. Type III_1 (the QFT case) is the most chaotic: the modular spectrum is all of R_+, the flow of weights is trivial and the algebra is 'maximally noncommutative'. This classification and the injective-factor uniqueness theorem are landmarks of noncommutative geometry.

concepts.name: Connes cocycle (Radon-Nikodym) flow
concepts.definition: For two states psi, phi on M (phi faithful normal) the Connes cocycle [Dpsi:Dphi]_t = Delta_{psi|phi}^{it} Delta_phi^{-it} is a one-parameter family of unitaries inside M relating their modular flows -- a noncommutative Radon-Nikodym derivative. Araki's relative entropy is its derivative at t=0. The cocycle flow ('half-sided boost' of one state relative to another) implements relative-entropy manipulations and appears in proofs of the QNEC, the ANEC and the algebraic Bekenstein bound.

concepts.name: Araki relative entropy
concepts.definition: S(psi||phi) = -<psi| log Delta_{psi|phi} |psi>, the relative entropy of two states on a von Neumann algebra defined via the relative modular operator without needing density matrices. Always non-negative, monotonic under inclusions of algebras (data processing), and finite even for type III algebras where ordinary entanglement entropy diverges. The state-independent, UV-finite quantity that survives in QFT and underpins the first law of entanglement, the JLMS relation and quantum energy inequalities.

concepts.name: Crossed product algebra
concepts.definition: The von Neumann algebra M x_sigma R obtained by adjoining to M the unitaries that implement an automorphism group (e.g. the modular flow), realized on H tensor L^2(R). Taking the crossed product of a type III_1 factor by its modular automorphism group yields a type II algebra (II_infinity, or II_1 when the extra clock has a bounded-below Hamiltonian). This is the algebraic mechanism by which adding an observer/clock with a Hamiltonian converts the trace-less type III QFT algebra into a type II algebra with a trace and finite entropy.

concepts.name: Observer (CLPW) crossed-product algebra
concepts.definition: Chandrasekaran-Longo-Penington-Witten: dressing QFT observables to the worldline of an observer carrying a clock (a bounded-below Hamiltonian) in a gravitational background produces a type II crossed-product algebra A_QFT x R. The observer's energy constraint plus the modular flow of the background state are exactly the data of the crossed product. For the de Sitter static patch this gives a type II_1 algebra with a maximal-entropy state (empty dS); for a black-hole/Killing horizon a type II_infinity algebra.

concepts.name: Type II trace and renormalized entropy
concepts.definition: A type II factor admits a (up to scale unique) faithful semifinite trace Tr, hence density matrices rho_psi defined by <psi|a|psi> = Tr(rho_psi a) and a von Neumann entropy S(psi) = -Tr(rho_psi log rho_psi). Because the trace fixes an arbitrary additive scale, the entropy is defined only up to a state-independent constant -- exactly the regularization-dependent constant in generalized entropy. The trace is what the crossed product manufactures out of the modular structure of a type III algebra.

concepts.name: Generalized entropy as von Neumann entropy
concepts.definition: S_gen = <A>/4G_N + S_out, the sum of horizon area in Planck units and exterior matter entropy. In the crossed-product (type II) algebra of gravitationally dressed observables the renormalized von Neumann entropy of a semiclassical state equals S_gen up to an additive constant. Individually the area term diverges as G->0 and S_out diverges from horizon entanglement (type III), but their sum -- the generalized entropy -- is finite and well-defined. The central bridge object linking modular theory, black-hole thermodynamics and holographic entropy.

concepts.name: QES / area operator from large-N algebra
concepts.definition: Chandrasekaran-Penington-Witten: the large-N single-trace algebra of a holographic CFT in the microcanonical ensemble is a type II_infinity factor whose central operator is the (renormalized) area of the black-hole bifurcation surface. The von Neumann entropy of semiclassical states is the generalized entropy, giving a Lorentzian, replica-free derivation of a special case of the quantum-extremal-surface (QES) prescription and of the quantum-corrected Bekenstein-Hawking formula.

concepts.name: JLMS relation
concepts.definition: Jafferis-Lewkowycz-Maldacena-Suh: the boundary (CFT) modular Hamiltonian of a region equals the bulk modular Hamiltonian of its entanglement wedge plus the area operator over 4G, K_bdy = A/4G + K_bulk. Boundary modular flow is dual to bulk modular flow. Recast algebraically, JLMS is the statement that the area operator is the central/affiliated operator distinguishing the type II crossed-product entropy from the bulk type III relative entropy; it makes generalized entropy the von Neumann entropy of the boundary algebra.

concepts.name: Bisognano-Wichmann theorem
concepts.definition: For the algebra of a Rindler wedge in the Minkowski vacuum, the Tomita-Takesaki modular flow is the geometric Lorentz boost preserving the wedge, and the modular conjugation is the wedge-reflecting CPT operator. Hence the vacuum restricted to a wedge is a KMS (thermal) state at the Unruh temperature, and the modular Hamiltonian is the boost generator. The prototype connecting abstract modular theory to physical geometry and the Unruh effect, and the seed of half-sided modular inclusion constructions.

concepts.name: Half-sided modular inclusion
concepts.definition: An inclusion N subset M of von Neumann algebras with a common cyclic-separating vector such that the modular flow of M maps N into itself for one sign of the parameter (Delta_M^{it} N Delta_M^{-it} subset N for t<=0). Borchers and Wiesbrock proved this forces a unique one-parameter translation group with positive generator satisfying [boost, translation] commutation relations -- i.e. a chunk of the Poincare/conformal group, hence emergent geometry and a positive 'energy', is reconstructed purely from two algebras and a state.

concepts.name: Emergent time from modular inclusions (Leutheusser-Liu)
concepts.definition: In the large-N limit of a holographic CFT in the thermofield-double state, the boundary type I algebra degenerates into an emergent type III_1 factor equipped with a half-sided modular translation. Leutheusser and Liu show the black-hole horizon, interior, infalling time and causal structure are all consequences of this type III_1 / half-sided-modular-inclusion structure -- bulk causal connectability behind the horizon emerges from boundary modular data.

concepts.name: Emergent type III_1 at large N
concepts.definition: At finite N the boundary CFT algebra is type I; in the strict large-N (G->0) limit of single-trace operators a type III_1 von Neumann algebra emerges, signalling a sharp horizon and the breakdown of a global tensor factorization. Including the first 1/N (gravitational) correction and the modular/ADM Hamiltonian promotes it to a type II crossed product with finite entropy. The type transition I -> III_1 -> II encodes the emergence of bulk locality, horizons and gravitational entropy.

concepts.name: Connes-Rovelli thermal time hypothesis
concepts.definition: In a generally covariant quantum theory there is no preferred external time; Connes and Rovelli propose that physical time flow is the modular flow of the actual (thermal) state of the system. The modular Hamiltonian of the state is, by Tomita-Takesaki, intrinsically defined and KMS at beta=1, so a thermodynamical state secretes its own time. Recovers the Unruh/Hawking temperatures and the Tolman-Ehrenfest relation, and connects modular theory to the problem of time in quantum gravity.

concepts.name: de Sitter static-patch observer algebra
concepts.definition: The observables of a static-patch observer in de Sitter, gravitationally dressed to the observer worldline, form a type II_1 crossed-product algebra. It has a maximal-entropy state corresponding to empty de Sitter (the cosmological-horizon Gibbons-Hawking entropy A/4G) and the entropy of any semiclassical state equals its generalized entropy. The cleanest closed-universe arena where an observer is necessary to even define gravitational entropy.

concepts.name: Quantum reference frames as crossed products
concepts.definition: De Vuyst-Eccles-Hoehn-Kirklin: a quantum reference frame (observer that may be in superposition) implementing gauge invariance is mathematically a crossed product. Different QRFs (different clocks, possibly with degenerate spectra, possibly many observers) give different type II algebras and therefore different entropies, so gravitational entropy is observer/frame-dependent. Unifies the relational-observables / problem-of-time picture with the modular-algebra picture of gravitational entropy.

concepts.name: Reeh-Schlieder theorem
concepts.definition: In QFT the vacuum (and any finite-energy state) is cyclic and separating for the algebra of any open region: acting with local operators in an arbitrarily small region can approximate any global state. This is the algebraic expression of vacuum entanglement and the precondition for Tomita-Takesaki theory to apply; it also forbids strictly local annihilation/creation of information and underlies the type III_1 nature of local algebras.

concepts.name: Algebraic ER=EPR
concepts.definition: Engelhardt-Liu: an algebraic formulation of ER=EPR in the G->0 limit in which bulk spacetime connectivity is encoded not just in the amount but in the algebraic structure of boundary entanglement (the type and commutant of the emergent algebra). At the Page time an emergent type III_1 subalgebra of high-complexity operators is transferred from the black hole to the radiation, recasting the information transition as an algebra-type transition.

concepts.name: Spacetime entanglement entropy / SJ state link
concepts.definition: Sorkin's spacetime entanglement entropy (SSEE) is computed from the Pauli-Jordan and Wightman two-point functions and yields an area law only after a geometric truncation of near-zero modes (otherwise a volume law). This sidesteps the lack of a Cauchy surface and is closely allied to the algebraic, region-based view of entropy in von Neumann algebras; the Sorkin-Johnston (SJ) state provides the covariant vacuum on which the construction rests, suggesting a discrete causal-set route to the type II/regulated entropy seen in modular constructions.

concepts.name: Emergent geometry from modular data
concepts.definition: The program of reconstructing spacetime structure -- causal order, horizons, a radial/holographic direction, even a slice of the Poincare or conformal group -- from purely algebraic modular data (modular flows, inclusions, cocycles). Half-sided modular inclusions yield translations with positive energy; modular Berry phases and modular chaos probe operator-algebra geometry; the emergence of a bulk radial direction is tied to non-compact support of spectral functions.

concepts.name: Subregion-subalgebra duality
concepts.definition: The holographic correspondence between a boundary subregion's algebra and the bulk algebra of its entanglement wedge: bulk operators are reconstructed as elements of the boundary subalgebra (entanglement-wedge reconstruction), and modular flows match (JLMS). Phrased algebraically it makes the von Neumann type and center of the subalgebra the carrier of bulk geometric information (wedge, horizon, area operator).

concepts.name: Corner non-geometricity of modular flow (dimensionally constrained)
concepts.definition: Project verdict (findings F-024 + F-026, rounds 9-12; VYPOCET-20 BD null-tip and VYPOCET-22 codim-2 joint) on whether the SSEE/SJ modular flow's failure of boost-geometricity at sharp entangling features -- the locus where the non-Hadamard corner anomaly of F-016/F-021 sits -- generalizes from 2D to 4D. The mechanism splits into two sub-parts with opposite fates. (1) The boost/slab sub-mechanism SURVIVES in 4D: the Benincasa-Dowker (BD) d'Alembertian object restores slab boost-geometricity, with the diagonal Bisognano-Wichmann boost-weight linearity holding at R^2=0.92 on a 4D Rindler wedge -- the off-diagonal slab/diamond slope contrast is also present and in the correct (2D) direction. (2) The corner-concentration sub-mechanism is robustly 2D-ONLY: it does NOT replicate in 4D. The BD null-tip probe (F-024) gives 3/5 signatures with the corner concentration absent, and the decisive codim-2 joint test (F-026, a flat 2-plane = Rindler-wedge edge where the boost Killing vector degenerates) REFUTES the generalization -- the nl-vs-edge slope is POSITIVE +0.25 (CI68 excludes 0), the SAME sign as the 4D null-tip (+0.71) and OPPOSITE the 2D corner (-0.383), so modular non-locality DECREASES toward the joint rather than concentrating. The cause is genuinely dimensional: a codim-2 locus in 4D has transverse y,z directions into which the boost flows freely even at the edge, absent in 2D where the corner is a point. Net: the corner sub-part of H4g-1/H5g-3 is dimensionally constrained to 2D (robust across 3 loci and 3 objects: link matrix, BD null-tip, BD codim-2 joint); the slab/boost sub-part is 4D-robust.

formulas.name: Tomita-Takesaki polar decomposition
formulas.latex: S\,a|\Omega\rangle = a^\dagger|\Omega\rangle,\qquad S = J\,\Delta^{1/2},\qquad \Delta^{it}\,M\,\Delta^{-it} = M,\quad J\,M\,J = M'
formulas.meaning: The Tomita operator S of a cyclic-separating vector polar-decomposes into the modular conjugation J and the positive modular operator Delta. Delta generates the modular automorphism of M; J maps M onto its commutant M'. The whole of modular theory follows from this single decomposition.

formulas.name: Modular flow and modular Hamiltonian
formulas.meaning: The modular automorphism group sigma_t with generator the modular Hamiltonian K = -log Delta. For a thermal/reduced state K is the entanglement Hamiltonian; modular flow is the intrinsic 'time' a state defines on its algebra.

formulas.name: KMS condition
formulas.meaning: Equilibrium analyticity at inverse temperature beta. Every cyclic-separating state is KMS at beta=1 with respect to its own modular flow, identifying modular time with thermal time and the vacuum-on-a-wedge with an Unruh-thermal state.

formulas.name: Bisognano-Wichmann modular Hamiltonian
formulas.latex: \Delta_W^{it} = U(\Lambda_W(2\pi t)),\qquad K_W = 2\pi\,\hat{B}_W,\qquad T_U = \frac{\hbar a}{2\pi c\,k_B}
formulas.meaning: For a Rindler wedge in the Minkowski vacuum the modular flow is the boost Lorentz transformation Lambda_W and the modular Hamiltonian is 2 pi times the boost generator B_W; the wedge state is thermal at the Unruh temperature T_U. The bridge from abstract modular theory to spacetime geometry.

formulas.name: Connes cocycle and Araki relative entropy
formulas.meaning: The Connes (Radon-Nikodym) cocycle relating the modular flows of two states lives inside M; Araki's relative entropy is built from the relative modular operator and is the t-derivative of the cocycle. UV-finite and state-independent, surviving even for type III algebras.

formulas.name: Crossed product converts type III_1 to type II
formulas.latex: \mathcal{N} = M \rtimes_{\sigma} \mathbb{R} \;\text{ on } \mathcal{H}\otimes L^2(\mathbb{R}),\qquad M\ \text{type III}_1 \;\Rightarrow\; \mathcal{N}\ \text{type II}
formulas.meaning: Adjoining the unitaries that implement the modular automorphism group sigma to a type III_1 factor M yields a type II crossed product N with a trace. Physically: adding an observer/clock with a Hamiltonian regulates the algebra so density matrices and entropy exist.

formulas.name: Type II trace and renormalized entropy
formulas.meaning: A type II factor's unique semifinite trace defines density matrices and a von Neumann entropy fixed only up to a state-independent additive constant -- exactly the renormalization ambiguity of generalized entropy.

formulas.name: Observer crossed-product entropy = generalized entropy
formulas.meaning: Dressing QFT observables to an observer with a clock gives a type II crossed product whose renormalized von Neumann entropy is the generalized entropy. The observer/QRF is the structure that makes gravitational entropy finite.

formulas.name: JLMS relation
formulas.meaning: Boundary modular Hamiltonian equals the area operator over 4G plus the bulk (entanglement-wedge) modular Hamiltonian; boundary and bulk relative entropies coincide. Algebraically the area operator is the central operator promoting bulk type III relative entropy to type II generalized entropy.

formulas.name: Half-sided modular inclusion / positive translation
formulas.meaning: Borchers-Wiesbrock: a half-sided modular inclusion forces a one-parameter translation group with a positive generator P obeying boost-translation commutation relations. Geometry and positive energy are reconstructed from two algebras and a vector -- the engine of emergent time and emergent horizons.

formulas.name: Connes modular spectrum invariant
formulas.meaning: The intersection over all faithful normal states of the modular spectra is a state-independent invariant; type III_1 (the QFT case) is characterized by S(M) = R_+ (all of the positive reals), the maximally chaotic class.

formulas.name: Sorkin spacetime entanglement entropy
formulas.meaning: SSEE from the generalized eigenvalues mu of the Wightman function W relative to the Pauli-Jordan (commutator) operator Delta_PJ. An area law follows only after a geometric truncation of near-zero modes; without truncation a volume law results. A causal-set/SJ-state route to regulated, region-based entropy.

formulas.name: Large-N algebra type transition
formulas.meaning: The boundary algebra runs from type I (finite N) to an emergent type III_1 (strict large N, sharp horizon) to type II (with the leading gravitational/observer correction), encoding the emergence of bulk locality, horizons and finite gravitational entropy.

references.title: Standard forms of von Neumann algebras (Quasi-standard von Neumann algebras)
references.significance: Original (unpublished/circulated) construction of the modular operator and conjugation, foundation of modular theory.

references.title: Tomita's Theory of Modular Hilbert Algebras and its Applications
references.significance: Rigorous formulation and dissemination of Tomita's modular theory, including the link to KMS states.

references.title: Une classification des facteurs de type III
references.significance: Classification of type III factors into III_lambda via the modular spectrum; cornerstone of noncommutative geometry.

references.title: An Algebraic Approach to Quantum Field Theory
references.significance: Founds algebraic QFT: physics encoded in a net of local von Neumann algebras rather than fields.

references.authors: J. J. Bisognano, E. H. Wichmann
references.title: On the Duality Condition for Quantum Fields
references.significance: Identifies the modular flow of a wedge algebra with the Lorentz boost; modular Hamiltonian = boost, vacuum = Unruh-thermal.

references.title: Scaling algebras and renormalization group in algebraic quantum field theory
references.significance: Scaling-algebra proof that local algebras are type III_1 whenever the theory has a nontrivial scaling limit (Buchholz-Fredenhagen-Haag circle).

references.title: On the modular structure of local algebras of observables
references.significance: Shows that in an asymptotically scale-invariant QFT the local algebras are type III_1, fixing the type of subregion algebras.

references.title: Half-sided modular inclusions of von-Neumann-algebras
references.significance: Introduces half-sided modular inclusions; with Borchers' theorem reconstructs translations with positive generator from modular data.

references.title: Von Neumann Algebra Automorphisms and Time-Thermodynamics Relation in General Covariant Quantum Theories
references.significance: Thermal time hypothesis: in a covariant theory physical time is the modular flow of the state; modular theory addresses the problem of time.

references.authors: D. L. Jafferis, A. Lewkowycz, J. Maldacena, S. J. Suh
references.title: Relative entropy equals bulk relative entropy
references.significance: JLMS relation K_bdy = A/4G + K_bulk; boundary modular flow dual to bulk modular flow, the algebraic seed of generalized entropy as von Neumann entropy.

references.title: Notes on Some Entanglement Properties of Quantum Field Theory
references.significance: Influential review introducing Tomita-Takesaki, type III algebras and Reeh-Schlieder to the high-energy community; reframes QFT entanglement as a property of algebras.

references.title: Causal connectability between quantum systems and the black hole interior in holographic duality
references.significance: Emergent type III_1 algebra and half-sided modular translation in large-N AdS/CFT explain the black-hole interior, horizon and infalling causal structure.

references.title: Emergent times in holographic duality
references.significance: Type III_1 structure and modular translations generate infalling time and the emergence of horizons/causal structure from boundary CFT data.

references.title: Gravity and the crossed product
references.significance: Shows the emergent type III_1 algebra of a black hole becomes type II_infinity as the crossed product by its modular automorphism group, giving a state-independent entropy.

references.authors: V. Chandrasekaran, R. Longo, G. Penington, E. Witten
references.title: An algebra of observables for de Sitter space
references.significance: Observer-dressed type II_1 crossed-product algebra for the de Sitter static patch; entropy of semiclassical states equals generalized entropy with a maximal-entropy empty-dS state.

references.authors: V. Chandrasekaran, G. Penington, E. Witten
references.title: Large N algebras and generalized entropy
references.significance: Constructs the type II_infinity large-N single-trace algebra whose von Neumann entropy is generalized entropy; Lorentzian, replica-free derivation of a special QES case.

references.authors: J. Kudler-Flam, S. Leutheusser, G. Satishchandran
references.title: Generalized Black Hole Entropy is von Neumann Entropy
references.significance: Structure theorem: dressed observables on any Killing horizon form a type II factor whose von Neumann entropy is generalized entropy, beyond the KMS case.

references.authors: J. De Vuyst, S. Eccles, P. A. Hoehn, J. Kirklin
references.title: Crossed products and quantum reference frames: on the observer-dependence of gravitational entropy
references.significance: Identifies quantum reference frames with crossed products; different QRFs give different type II algebras and entropies, so gravitational entropy is observer-dependent.

references.title: Algebraic ER=EPR and Complexity Transfer
references.significance: Algebraic ER=EPR: bulk connectivity encoded in algebra structure; Page-time transfer of an emergent type III_1 high-complexity subalgebra from black hole to radiation.

references.authors: R. D. Sorkin, Y. K. Yazdi
references.title: Entanglement Entropy in Causal Set Theory
references.significance: Defines spacetime entanglement entropy from Pauli-Jordan and Wightman functions; area law only after geometric truncation of near-zero modes (otherwise volume law).

references.authors: M. Saravani, R. D. Sorkin, Y. K. Yazdi
references.title: Spacetime entanglement entropy in 1+1 dimensions
references.significance: Covariant spacetime formulation of entanglement entropy avoiding a Cauchy slice, recovering the area law with the truncation prescription.

references.authors: A. Belenchia, D. M. T. Benincasa, M. Letizia, S. Liberati
references.title: On the Entanglement Entropy of Quantum Fields in Causal Sets
references.significance: Computes SSEE for the Sorkin-Johnston state on causal sets, exhibiting truncation-dependent area vs volume law in a discrete background. [Authors corrected 2026-06-07: previous entry 'N. Surya, N. X, Y. K. Yazdi' was a placeholder; actual authors Belenchia–Benincasa–Letizia–Liberati confirmed via arXiv.]

references.title: Relative Entropy of States of von Neumann Algebras
references.significance: Defines relative entropy via the relative modular operator without density matrices; finite and monotonic even for type III algebras.

references.title: On revolutionizing quantum field theory with Tomita's modular theory
references.significance: Review establishing the Borchers commutation theorem linking modular flow to translations, basis of half-sided modular inclusions and emergent geometry.

references.title: Entropy of Coherent Excitations
references.significance: Sharpens the algebraic relative-entropy and Bekenstein-bound program using modular theory; input to the entropy formulas of observer algebras.

references.title: Relative entropy and the Bekenstein bound
references.significance: Derives the Bekenstein bound from positivity/monotonicity of relative entropy, a model for how modular/algebraic quantities yield physical bounds.

references.authors: F. Ciolli, R. Longo, G. Ruzzi
references.title: The information in a wave
references.significance: Computes von Neumann/relative entropy of wave packets in QFT via modular theory, illustrating finite algebraic entropy in a type III setting.

references.authors: K. Jensen, J. Sorce, A. J. Speranza
references.title: Generalized entropy for general subregions in quantum gravity
references.significance: Extends the crossed-product construction to general subregions, associating a type II algebra and a renormalized generalized entropy to arbitrary regions.

references.title: Crossed product algebras and generalized entropy for subregions
references.significance: Develops crossed-product subregion algebras and their generalized entropy, broadening the observer-algebra program beyond horizons.

references.title: Lectures on entanglement, von Neumann algebras, and emergence of spacetime
references.significance: Pedagogical synthesis of modular theory, type III/II algebras, crossed products and emergent spacetime as of 2025. [Author corrected 2026-06-07: paper is by Hong Liu, not S. Leutheusser — confirmed via arXiv:2510.07017.]

references.authors: J. van der Heijden, E. Verlinde
references.title: An operator algebraic approach to black hole information
references.significance: Operator-algebraic account of the black-hole information problem using Jones index theory of type II_1 subfactors; information recovery via Jones basic construction. [Corrected 2026-06-07: JHEP02(2025)207 belongs to van der Heijden–Verlinde (arXiv:2408.00071). Previous entry misidentified authors as Ali Ahmad–Klinger–Lin. Separate Ali Ahmad–Klinger–Lin paper is arXiv:2407.01695.]

references.authors: S. Ali Ahmad, M. S. Klinger, S. Lin
references.title: Semifinite von Neumann algebras in gauge theory and gravity
references.significance: Studies semifinite conditions for crossed products of type III algebras with locally compact groups; derives a sufficient condition for semifiniteness implying centrality of the modular flow. [Added 2026-06-07: this is the correct Ali Ahmad–Klinger–Lin paper; previous entry mistakenly pointed to van der Heijden–Verlinde JHEP paper.]

references.title: Gravitational algebras and the generalized second law
references.significance: Derives the generalized second law for arbitrary cuts of Killing horizons from crossed-product gravitational algebras using monotonicity of relative entropy; Lorentzian, algebraic derivation. [Corrected 2026-06-07: DOI 10.1007/JHEP11(2024)099 belongs to Faulkner–Speranza (arXiv:2405.00847), NOT to Kudler-Flam–Leutheusser–Satishchandran. Previous entry misattributed both authors and arXiv ID.]

openProblems.statement: Whether the type II description (entropy up to an additive constant, no pure states) is fundamental or an approximation to an underlying type I theory that resolves black-hole/de Sitter microstates.
openProblems.whyHard: Type II has no minimal projections, so it cannot count microstates; recovering type I (a definite Hilbert space of states) presumably requires non-perturbative quantum gravity that fixes the additive constant and the number of states e^{S}.
openProblems.attempts: Type I approximations of the crossed product, ensemble/baby-universe interpretations of the additive constant, microcanonical and stretched-horizon refinements.

openProblems.statement: What objective, frame-independent content gravitational entropy has, given that different observers / quantum reference frames yield different type II algebras and different entropies.
openProblems.whyHard: Entropy differences and relative entropies are frame-covariant, but the absolute entropy and even the algebra type depend on the chosen QRF, clock spectrum and dressing; there is no agreed notion of a preferred frame in a closed universe.
openProblems.attempts: Perspective-neutral QRF formalism (De Vuyst-Eccles-Hoehn-Kirklin), comparison of dressings, focus on relative entropy as the invariant content.

openProblems.statement: Compute and interpret modular flow for generic regions/states where it is nonlocal and non-geometric, as needed to reconstruct bulk geometry and prove energy conditions.
openProblems.whyHard: Outside wedges, conformal cases and a few exactly solvable models the modular Hamiltonian is a nonlocal operator with no closed form; its flow mixes points and scales unpredictably.
openProblems.attempts: Modular Berry phase / modular chaos, half-sided inclusion techniques, perturbation around the vacuum, numerical/lattice modular Hamiltonians.

openProblems.statement: Reconstruct full bulk spacetime geometry (metric, causal structure, radial direction) purely from algebraic modular data (flows, inclusions, cocycles).
openProblems.whyHard: Half-sided modular inclusions yield only translations and a positive generator; obtaining a full metric and the holographic radial coordinate requires extra spectral input (non-compact support) whose origin is unclear.
openProblems.attempts: Borchers-Wiesbrock translations, Leutheusser-Liu emergent-time program, spectral-function/radial-direction analyses, modular-chaos diagnostics.

openProblems.statement: Whether the type III_1 property and the associated lack of factorization are obstructions to a tensor-factorized Hilbert space of gravity, or are removed in the full theory.
openProblems.whyHard: Type III_1 forbids a global tensor split and local density matrices; whether quantum gravity restores factorization (edge modes, soft hair, discreteness) or keeps it intrinsically non-factorized is unsettled.
openProblems.attempts: Edge-mode / extended-Hilbert-space constructions, split property, discreteness (causal sets, LQG area gap) as a physical UV cutoff.

openProblems.statement: Justify and derive the geometric truncation needed for the Sorkin spacetime entanglement entropy to obey an area law, and relate it to the type II/regulated entropy of modular constructions.
openProblems.whyHard: The near-zero-mode truncation is presently a prescription chosen to recover the area law; its physical origin and its precise correspondence to the crossed-product regularization are not established.
openProblems.attempts: Causal-set SSEE calculations, Sorkin-Johnston state, comparison with continuum modular entropy and the LQG area gap as candidate physical cutoffs.

openProblems.statement: Clarify the physical status of the de Sitter type II_1 maximal-entropy state and whether a single static-patch observer suffices to define cosmological entropy and dynamics.
openProblems.whyHard: The construction needs an observer inside a closed universe with finite-dimensional-like (II_1) entropy; the role of multiple observers, the global state and the cosmological horizon's microstates is unclear.
openProblems.attempts: Multi-observer QRF crossed products, covariant-observer algebras, Janus deformations and transitions in gravitational algebras.

openProblems.statement: Whether the thermal time hypothesis has genuine empirical content beyond reproducing Unruh/Hawking temperatures, i.e. whether modular flow is the physical time in realistic non-equilibrium settings.
openProblems.whyHard: Generic physical states are not KMS and have nonlocal, state-dependent modular flow that need not match clock-measured proper time; matching modular time to laboratory time in non-stationary situations is unproven.
openProblems.attempts: Tolman-Ehrenfest derivations, epistemic analyses, links to the problem of time and to crossed-product clocks.

connections.description: Modular theory is the mathematical backbone of entanglement-from-geometry: the modular Hamiltonian, JLMS relation, first law of entanglement and the crossed-product / type II_infinity area operator all live here. The 2022+ observer-algebra revolution made generalized entropy the von Neumann entropy of a type II algebra, sharpening Van Raamsdonk-style 'entanglement builds spacetime'. The densest and most active bridge.

Witten's crossed product turns the emergent type III_1 black-hole algebra into type II_infinity with a state-independent entropy; Kudler-Flam-Leutheusser-Satishchandran prove generalized black-hole entropy is von Neumann entropy on any Killing horizon. Algebraic ER=EPR recasts the Page-time information transition as the transfer of an emergent type III_1 subalgebra. Resolves firewall/interior puzzles via half-sided modular translations.

Leutheusser-Liu derive the emergent type III_1 algebra and infalling time of the eternal AdS black hole from boundary CFT modular data; Chandrasekaran-Penington-Witten obtain a type II_infinity large-N algebra whose entropy is the generalized entropy, a replica-free QES derivation. Subregion-subalgebra duality and JLMS make the von Neumann type the carrier of bulk geometric information. The fastest-growing crossroads.

The crossed-product / type II entropy is computed in the G->0 limit of Lorentzian effective field theory on a fixed semiclassical background; Bisognano-Wichmann modular flow reproduces the Unruh effect and the thermal time hypothesis recovers Hawking/Unruh temperatures from state-intrinsic modular flow. Modular theory thus organizes QFT-in-curved-spacetime entanglement and energy conditions.

Quantum reference frames are crossed products (De Vuyst-Eccles-Hoehn-Kirklin), so the observer/relational-observables and problem-of-time issues are the same algebraic structure that renders gravitational entropy finite and observer-dependent. The thermal time hypothesis gives a modular answer to the problem of time, and the type III_1 obstruction to factorization sharpens background-independence and the role of the observer.

Sorkin's spacetime entanglement entropy (SSEE), built from the Pauli-Jordan and Wightman functions of the covariant Sorkin-Johnston state, yields an area law only after a geometric truncation of near-zero modes -- structurally parallel to how the crossed product regulates the type III entropy of a region. A discrete causal-set route to the regulated, region-based (type II-like) entropy of modular constructions; almost unexplored as an explicit correspondence.

Type III_1 makes subregion entanglement entropy UV-divergent, demanding a physical cutoff; LQG's discrete area spectrum and nonzero area gap (Delta-A ~ l_Pl^2) is a candidate microscopic regulator that could play the role the crossed-product trace plays algebraically, fixing the additive constant in generalized entropy. The link between the area gap and the type II trace/area operator is suggestive but essentially unworked.

Connes' classification of type III factors, the modular spectrum, the Connes cocycle and the time-thermodynamics (thermal time) relation are foundational results of noncommutative geometry imported wholesale into quantum gravity. The same operator-algebra toolkit (modular automorphisms, crossed products, KMS) underlies both Connes' spectral geometry and the observer-algebra program. A deep, mostly latent shared-mathematics bridge.

This project's own numerical findings establish the link by data: a geometric/spectral truncation of the Sorkin-Johnston spacetime entanglement entropy (SSEE) realizes the type III_1 -> type II algebra transition. F-015 (2D causal diamonds): two of three vN-type proxies confirm it -- an 80x collapse of the entropy trace (S_full ~ N^1.04 volume-law -> S_trunc saturating area/log-law) plus a modular spectrum going flat-dense (Connes III_1) to integrable with a sharp IR edge (type II); the type lives in the STATE/entropy, not in the Pauli-Jordan kinematics. F-019 (4D box-slab): all three proxies pass, with the n_max = 2 N^{3/4} rank truncation acting as an operative crossed-product-style observer regulator giving a 4D area law S ~ sqrt(N). F-023 (2D de Sitter static patch): the discrete SJ probe resolves the CLPW type II_1 (bounded patch, finite trace) vs type II_infinity distinction via content saturation. F-025 (rounds 9-12, 4D dS sech^2-weighted slab): the II_1 vs II_infinity CONTENT discriminator lifts cleanly to the 4D dS patch -- N_total saturates to a finite cap (480, R^2=1.000) for the bounded dS patch versus flat linear growth for the unbounded control, while the truncated 4D area-law channel (n_max=2N^{3/4}) shows a real but UNSATURATED separation signal at N<=2500 (dS R*-exponent 0.27 vs flat 0.52, slope ratio ~3x) -- a real 4D-specific signal that does not yet plateau within dense-eigh budget. F-027 (rounds 9-12, 2D high-density retest to rho up to 1e4): the max-entropy TRACIAL (type II_1) signature is in-principle INVISIBLE to the discrete SJ + kappa-truncation probe -- the tracial state falls into a spectral gap between the untruncated III_1 UV pile-up (eps->0) and the kappa-truncated type-II IR gap (eps >~ 2.7), so the kappa = sqrt(N)/(4pi) cutoff systematically removes the low-eps modes that would carry it; the II_1 identification therefore rests on content saturation, not a direct tracial signature, and this honest null is closed at density up to rho ~ 1e4. The causal-set SSEE truncation is thus a concrete discrete realization of the modular/crossed-product regularization of type III entropy. ROZSIRENO O TRI NEJNOVEJSI NALEZY (F-029 / F-031 / F-032, rounds 13-15): (1) F-032 -- 2D-diamant proxy3 (central-sequences / self-averaging) se pri >=30 seedech stava SIGNIFIKANTNIM (50 seedu slope = -0.224 +/- 0.069, t=3.25, CI [-0.30,-0.15] vylucuje 0), takze 2D verdikt se UPGRADUJE z 2/3 (F-015) na 3/3 -- III_1 -> II prechod podporen vsemi tremi nezavislymi proxy v 2D. (2) F-029 -- 2D area-zakon konstanta c ~ 7.6-7.7 robustni pres 5x rho a 3.6x patch-size, cross-HW reprodukovana (publication-strength). (3) F-031 -- POCTIVY NEGATIV: ve 4D (plocha-kauzalni + dS-sech^2 konstrukce) genuinne NEEXISTUJE area-zakon S ~ A_proper (S_full ~ rho^0.997 objemove vs korigovana kodim-2 plocha A_mol ~ rho^0.494, zadny pomer rho-invariantni; 2D funguje jen proto, ze horizont je bod a oba ~rho^1; konformni kaveat: 4D bezhmotny skalar neni konformne invariantni). ZAVER: hrana opravnene drzi partially -- 2D je publication-strength (3/3 proxy + area-zakon), 4D area-zakon je cisty negativ, ale II_1/II_inf content-saturace se do 4D zveda cleanly (F-025, N_total cap=480, R^2=1.000).

The Chandrasekaran-Longo-Penington-Witten observer algebra for the de Sitter static patch (arXiv:2206.10780) is a type II_1 crossed product whose maximal-entropy state is empty de Sitter (cosmological-horizon Gibbons-Hawking entropy A/4G); a single static-patch observer is necessary even to DEFINE gravitational entropy in a closed/cosmological spacetime, making the modular-algebra picture directly a quantum-cosmology statement about horizon entropy and the wave function in a finite causal patch. This project's F-023 confirms by data that a discrete Sorkin-Johnston probe on a 2D de Sitter static patch SEES the bounded-patch finite-trace type II_1 signature (content saturation to a finite cap) versus the unbounded type II_infinity flat control -- a numerical bridge from CLPW de Sitter algebras to causal-set cosmology.

Chandrasekaran-Penington-Witten (arXiv:2209.10454) build the large-N single-trace algebra of a holographic CFT in the microcanonical ensemble as a type II_infinity crossed-product factor whose central operator is the renormalized area of the bifurcation surface and whose von Neumann entropy is the generalized entropy -- a Lorentzian, replica-free derivation of a special case of the quantum-extremal-surface prescription. The crossed product (adjoining the modular/ADM Hamiltonian to the emergent type III_1 boundary algebra) is the algebraic mechanism turning holographic entanglement into a finite, observer-relative entropy; subregion-subalgebra duality and JLMS make the von Neumann type the carrier of bulk geometric information.
